title: Intercontinental Playoffs Set for March 26 & 31 in Mexico
detail: The FIFA World Cup 2026 intercontinental playoffs are scheduled for March 26 and 31 at two Mexican venues: Estadio Guadalajara (Estadio Akron, 48,071 capacity) and Estadio Monterrey (Estadio BBVA, 53,500 capacity). Six teams compete in two brackets of three: DR Congo (Africa), Iraq (Asia), New Caledonia (Oceania), Bolivia (CONMEBOL), Jamaica, and Suriname (CONCACAF). Winners qualify for the tournament. The format uses single-leg knockout matches with extra time and penalties.

title: UEFA World Cup Playoffs: 16 Teams Compete for 4 Spots (March 26 & 31)
detail: UEFA's World Cup qualification playoffs are also scheduled for March 26 and 31, 2026, with 16 European teams competing for the final 4 UEFA spots at the World Cup. Combined with the intercontinental playoffs, the final qualification picture for the 48-team tournament will be complete by April 2026. The tournament kicks off June 11, 2026 in Mexico City with the final on July 19, 2026.
